bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am opening with a high-utility bid targeting my maximum possible utility. Since there is no evidence about the opponent’s priorities, I should start by anchoring on my top value for every issue and only concede later if their bids reveal tradeoffs.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only small concessions from my opening offer. The opponent’s first offer is quite low for me and shows no meaningful concession pattern yet, so I concede only on lower-weight issues while holding firm on the most important issueE and other high-weight items.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession from my last offer to avoid standing completely still. The opponent has only moved slightly on issueE, and their bids remain very low utility for me, so I am holding firm on the most important issues issueE, issueB, and issueD while conceding modestly on low-weight issueA.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, while making a small concession on low-weight issueC toward the opponent’s repeated valueB. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and show no reliable concession pattern, especially after reverting on issueE, so I am holding firm on my most important issues B, D, and E.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.79 by conceding mainly on my low-weight issues, issueA and issueC, where the opponent has repeatedly signaled valueA/valueB. Since it is still very early and the opponent’s offers remain low for me with little real concession, I am holding firm on my highest-weight issues issueE, issueB, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.79 of my weighted scale, since it is still very early at t=0.10 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has repeated nearly the same low-utility offer for me and has not shown meaningful concession, so I will hold firm on my most important issues while making a small non-repetitive concession on issueD.

The opponent’s latest valid offer gives me about 0.793 utility, which is slightly better for me than my previous bid and preserves top values on my most important issues B, D, and E. Although it is still early, they have clearly moved to a bid I should be willing to accept, with concessions mainly on my lower-weight issues A and C. With no natural-language messages to weigh, I will mirror their latest offer to secure a strong agreement.
